danger_signals.visual_form: Hostile entities populating stages; visual distinction from navigable space
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring avoidance or defeat

danger_signals.visual_form: Major enemy figures occupying entire stage or positioned at stage terminus
danger_signals.meaning: Significant progression barrier; heightened danger requiring character-specific strategy
Passive danger; collision results in damage

danger_signals.visual_form: Character health bar (max 10 hit points) decreasing; potential visual darkening/damage state
danger_signals.meaning: Proximity to character defeat; urgency to collect health pickups

danger_signals.visual_form: Sentinel manufacturing facility and Avalon corrupted by alien technorganic presence
danger_signals.meaning: World-state threat: assimilation risk to all inhabitants; existential danger level

safety_signals.meaning: Progress checkpoint; temporary safety from current threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Wall-cling, flight, double-jump, ceiling-traverse visual states
safety_signals.meaning: Character-specific safe passage; access to shortcuts and protected areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Ability to swap playable character after stage completion or life loss
safety_signals.meaning: Strategic reset; access to character better suited to upcoming challenge

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: X insignia (implied through playable character roster: Beast, Psylocke, Gambit, Nightcrawler, Wolverine, Cyclops)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Phalanx clones of X-Men appear as later-stage enemies (endgame confrontation with clone factory variants)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stages progress through X-Men operational theaters: Sentinel facility, Avalon, Apocalypse's facility, Savage Land, Phalanx ship, clone factory, Nexus

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Technorganic alien entity identity; visual manifestation through contamination of facilities
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard Phalanx enemies; Phalanx clones of named characters (Brainchild clone, X-Men clones); infection/assimilation visual theme
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sentinel manufacturing facility (primary contamination site), Avalon (secondary spread), Apocalypse's facility (permitted spread), Phalanx ship (command structure), clone factory (mass production)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Master of magnetism; electromagnetic visual identity
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Initially antagonistic; becomes ally-faction after detection of Phalanx threat; playable character (unlockable after level 3) with unique electromagnetic attack visual
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Avalon (home base); shifts from enemy presence to ally cooperation during Phalanx crisis

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Major boss encounter; visual distinctness indicating power level above standard enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Apocalypse's facility (stage location); permitted Phalanx virus spread indicates temporary alignment with Phalanx threat rather than X-Men values

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Minor boss encounter on Phalanx ship
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Phalanx ship (operating location); indicates alien/cosmic threat tier

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sentinel manufacturing facility operates; Avalon stable under Magneto/Exodus control
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Standard enemy patrols; localized hazards; Professor X monitors via Cerebro; no assimilation pressure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sentinel facility visually corrupted; technorganic infection visible in environmental design; enemy composition shifts to include Phalanx variants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Professor X initiates X-Men deployment; enemies become more aggressive; health pickup scarcity increases tension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Secondary location contamination; Magneto's fortress compromised; visual complexity increases (layered threat visualization)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Magneto forced into alliance with X-Men; stage difficulty escalates; clone variants appear; assimilation mechanics become visible threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ancient facility allows Phalanx presence; visual fusion of old-world and technorganic aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Major boss fight; Apocalypse confrontation indicates turning point; player forced to adapt strategy

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Phalanx mass assimilation in Savage Land
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Biological/primal environment corrupted by technorganic presence; clone factory visual prominence; Brainchild clone encounter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Clone enemies proliferate; character doubles suggest identity threat; higher enemy density; health pickups become critical

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Final confrontation—Phalanx ship and clone nexus
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien spacecraft interior; clone production facility fully visible; X-Men clones appear as enemy variants; highest visual density of threat indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player faces clones of own team; identity/assimilation threat peak; Deathbird minor boss indicates cosmic scale; endgame difficulty highest

environmental_storytelling.example: Sentinel facility (industrial threat) → Avalon (political threat through Magneto) → Apocalypse's facility (ideological threat) → Savage Land (biological/primal threat) → Phalanx ship (cosmic/existential threat) → clone factory (identity threat). Each environment visually reinforces narrative stakes.
Early stages contain generic enemies; mid-game introduces Phalanx-contaminated variants; endgame features X-Men clones. Enemy visual mutation signals progression toward assimilation endpoint.

Wall-cling (Beast, Psylocke, Wolverine), flight (Magneto), teleportation (Nightcrawler), double-jump (Nightcrawler, Psylocke) unlock stage shortcuts. Inaccessible areas visually indicate character-specific story paths and environmental layout as tutorial of team composition.
Early stages contain abundant pickups; late stages become sparse. Visual resource availability communicates threat escalation and urgency without explicit narrative.
Minor bosses appear at stage terminus; major bosses occupy entire stages. Visual real estate allocation indicates narrative importance and difficulty tier.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Enhanced mutant attack power active; visual brightness/clarity; optimal offensive capability
Reduced mutant attack power; visual darkening/damage state; forced defensive playstyle; critical dependency on health pickups
Unique vulnerability: cannot melee; reliance on ranged electromagnetic blast barrage; position-dependent tactical play
Hostile engagement with player character; visual distinction from environmental hazards; defeat-able through character melee/ranged attacks
Mirror player character abilities; visual echo of playable character models; assimilation theme made visceral through identity doubling; heightened mechanical threat
Communication/narration only; provides plot exposition; emotional/strategic justification for stage progression; establishes moral authority for X-Men faction
Visual/mechanical shift: becomes playable; suggests faction realignment under existential threat; political/pragmatic alliance over ideological opposition

notes: X-Men 2: Clone Wars communicates world-state primarily through environmental progression and enemy composition escalation. The narrative arc—Phalanx detection → facility contamination → spread to Avalon → assimilation mass production → clone nexus confrontation—is mirrored in visual facility transformation and enemy variant density. Character selection mechanics serve dual narrative/mechanical function: team composition implies X-Men organizational structure; traversal ability gating reinforces individual character identity. The Magneto alliance (unlockable at level 3, forced cooperation by level 3+) visualizes political pragmatism under existential threat. Clone enemies at endgame represent assimilation threat made tangible through identity doubling. Health bar system (10 HP maximum) creates constant visual feedback of character vulnerability. No explicit faction color-coding observed in available documentation; faction identity relies on narrative context, character roster distinctness, and environmental association rather than chromatic coding.
